page.content: In the following analysis, we present a comprehensive overview of the data concerning the detection of Operational Taxonomic Units (OTUs) across various groups, offering insights into the temporal dynamics of lung cancer screening methodologies.

page.content: By comparing baseline screening results from lung cancer patients (LCs, n = 13) to those observed during annual screenings (LCs, n = 21), we aim to elucidate the impact of longitudinal assessment on microbial diversity. This analysis is pivotal in informing future research directions and clinical practices, as it highlights the importance of recurrent cross-sectional analysis and trajectory analysis in identifying significant changes in microbial communities. Ultimately, these findings contribute to a nuanced understanding of how screening processes influence patient outcomes and the overall trajectory of disease management.

lines.text: By comparing baseline screening results from lung cancer patients (LCs, n = 13) to those observed during annual screenings
(LCs, n = 21), we aim to elucidate the impact of longitudinal assessment on microbial diversity. This analysis is pivotal in
informing future research directions and clinical practices, as it highlights the importance of recurrent cross-sectional analysis
and trajectory analysis in identifying significant changes in microbial communities. Ultimately, these findings contribute to a
nuanced understanding of how screening processes influence patient outcomes and the overall trajectory of disease
